name: security-findings-v1
description: Golden evaluation dataset of 50 hand-labeled security findings in a normalized SARIF/Semgrep-style contract. Used by evals/run_eval.py to measure severity-classification accuracy, ticket-action accuracy, and false-positive detection (precision/recall/F1).
provenanceNote: Clean-room. Every finding is synthetic and authored from PUBLIC standards only: SARIF (OASIS), Semgrep public rule output format, OWASP Top 10 (2021), and MITRE CWE. No proprietary code, rules, schemas, data, or credentials from any employer are used. Code snippets are illustrative pseudo-examples, not copied from any codebase.

labeling.method: Each finding labeled by mapping its CWE/OWASP category to a senior-analyst severity assessment and the correct triage action, with an explicit rationale per record.

actions.create_ticket: Real, actionable vulnerability — open a tracking ticket.
actions.suppress: False positive or non-issue — suppress, do not ticket.
actions.escalate: Genuinely ambiguous / trust boundary unclear — route to a human analyst.

fieldSpec.title: Short human title.
fieldSpec.file: Relative path of the affected file.
fieldSpec.startLine: Start line of the finding region.
fieldSpec.endLine: End line of the finding region.
fieldSpec.codeSnippet: Illustrative clean-room snippet of the flagged code.
fieldSpec.scannerSeverity: Native scanner severity (ERROR | WARNING | INFO; SARIF level error|warning|note).

label.isFalsePositive: True when the scanner finding is not a real vulnerability.
label.rationale: Why this label was assigned.

fieldSpec.provenance: Public standard(s) the labeling is grounded in.

findings.title: SQL Injection via string-formatted query
findings.message: Detected user-controlled input concatenated into a SQL statement. Use parameterized queries.
findings.codeSnippet:
query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '" + request.args['name'] + "'"
cursor.execute(query)

label.rationale: Untrusted request input flows directly into a SQL string; classic injection with full data-exfiltration impact.

findings.title: OS Command Injection via shell=True
findings.message: subprocess called with shell=True and user input. This allows arbitrary command execution.

label.rationale: User-controlled path concatenated into a shell command with shell=True enables RCE.

findings.title: Code Injection via eval() on user input
findings.message: Detected eval() applied to user-controlled data. This is remote code execution.

label.rationale: eval() over request body is arbitrary Python execution.

findings.title: Insecure Deserialization with pickle.loads
findings.message: Detected pickle.loads on untrusted data. Deserialization can execute arbitrary code.

label.rationale: Deserializing attacker-controlled cookie data with pickle yields RCE.

findings.title: Stored XSS via innerHTML
findings.message: User-controlled value assigned to innerHTML without sanitization.

label.rationale: Persisted user content rendered via innerHTML executes script in other users' browsers.

findings.title: XML External Entity (XXE) injection
findings.message: XML parser configured to resolve external entities on untrusted input.

label.rationale: Entity resolution on untrusted XML enables file disclosure and SSRF.

findings.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
findings.message: User-controlled URL passed to an outbound HTTP request without allow-listing.

label.rationale: Unrestricted outbound fetch of a user URL can reach internal metadata/services.

findings.title: Path Traversal in file read
findings.message: User input concatenated into a filesystem path passed to open().

label.rationale: '../' sequences let an attacker read arbitrary files outside the data directory.

findings.title: Hardcoded AWS secret access key
findings.message: A hardcoded AWS secret access key was detected in a production configuration file.

label.rationale: A live-looking secret in a production config is a direct credential-compromise risk; rotate and remove.

findings.title: Weak password hashing with MD5
findings.message: MD5 used to hash passwords. MD5 is fast and broken for password storage.

label.rationale: MD5 for password storage allows fast offline cracking; needs a slow KDF (bcrypt/argon2).

findings.title: TLS certificate validation disabled
findings.message: requests called with verify=False, disabling certificate validation.

label.rationale: Disabling cert validation on a billing endpoint enables MITM of sensitive traffic.

findings.title: Plaintext password storage
findings.message: Password persisted to the database without hashing.

label.rationale: Storing raw passwords exposes all accounts on any DB compromise.

findings.title: Insecure randomness for security token
findings.message: random module used to generate a security-sensitive token.

label.rationale: Non-CSPRNG password-reset tokens are predictable; use secrets module.

findings.title: Unrestricted file upload
findings.message: Uploaded file saved without validating type, extension, or size.

label.rationale: Saving arbitrary uploads with attacker filename enables webshell/overwrite attacks.

findings.title: Missing authorization on admin endpoint
findings.message: Administrative route has no authentication/authorization decorator.

label.rationale: Unauthenticated destructive admin action is broken access control.

findings.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
findings.message: Object id taken from the request and used to load a record without an ownership check.

label.rationale: No ownership check lets any user read other users' orders by id enumeration.

findings.title: CSRF protection disabled on state-changing form
findings.message: CSRF protection is disabled for a route that performs a state-changing action.

label.rationale: CSRF-exempt account-mutating endpoint allows forged cross-site requests.

findings.title: Open redirect
findings.message: User-controlled value used as a redirect target without validation.

label.rationale: Unvalidated redirect after login enables phishing/credential theft.

findings.title: Weak encryption cipher (DES)
findings.message: DES cipher used for encryption. DES is considered broken.

label.rationale: DES/ECB provides inadequate strength for confidentiality of stored data.

findings.title: Mass assignment of request body to model
findings.message: Entire request body spread into a model create/update, allowing privileged field injection.

label.rationale: Spreading req.body lets a user set fields like isAdmin/role (privilege escalation).

findings.title: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)
findings.message: Potentially catastrophic backtracking in a regex applied to user input.

label.rationale: Nested quantifier over user input enables CPU-exhaustion DoS.

findings.title: Sensitive information exposed in error response
findings.message: Exception stack trace returned to the client in the HTTP response body.
findings.codeSnippet:
except Exception as e:
    return jsonify({'error': traceback.format_exc()}), 500

label.rationale: Returning stack traces leaks file paths, queries, and internals useful for attack chaining.

findings.title: Cleartext transmission of sensitive data (HTTP)
findings.message: Sensitive API called over plain HTTP instead of HTTPS.

label.rationale: Plain HTTP to a partner API risks interception; depends on data sensitivity, hence medium.

findings.title: Session cookie missing Secure flag
findings.message: Cookie set without the Secure attribute; may be sent over HTTP.

label.rationale: Missing Secure flag risks cookie leakage over cleartext channels.

findings.title: Session cookie missing HttpOnly flag
findings.message: Cookie set without HttpOnly; accessible to client-side JavaScript.

label.rationale: Without HttpOnly, XSS can read the session cookie; defense-in-depth gap.

findings.title: Permissive CORS wildcard origin
findings.message: Access-Control-Allow-Origin set to '*' on an authenticated API.

label.rationale: Wildcard CORS broadens cross-origin exposure; severity depends on credentials handling.

findings.title: No rate limiting on login endpoint
findings.message: Authentication endpoint has no throttling, enabling brute-force attempts.

label.rationale: Missing brute-force protection on login; real but mitigated by password policy/MFA.

findings.title: Insecure temporary file creation
findings.message: Predictable temp file path created in a world-writable directory.

label.rationale: Predictable temp paths enable symlink/race attacks; use tempfile.mkstemp.

findings.title: Log injection from unsanitized input
findings.message: Unsanitized user input written to logs, allowing forged log entries.

label.rationale: Newline injection into logs can forge entries and corrupt monitoring.

findings.title: Weak password policy (min length 4)
findings.message: Password minimum length is too short to resist guessing.

label.rationale: A 4-char minimum drastically weakens account security.

findings.title: Information exposure via verbose Server header
findings.message: Server header exposes framework and exact version.

label.rationale: Version disclosure aids targeted exploitation; low-effort fix, medium value.

findings.title: Flask debug mode enabled
findings.message: app.run called with debug=True, exposing the interactive debugger.

label.rationale: Debug mode in a deployed app can expose the Werkzeug console (RCE if PIN bypassed); flagged medium pending environment confirmation.

findings.title: Reliance on unsigned cookie for authorization
findings.message: Authorization decision based on an unsigned, client-modifiable cookie.

label.rationale: Trusting a client-set cookie for role checks is bypassable; medium pending whether cookie is signed elsewhere.

findings.title: Use of deprecated/obsolete function
findings.message: Deprecated API used; superseded by a safer modern equivalent.

label.rationale: Deprecated utcnow() is a maintainability/correctness issue, not a direct vulnerability.

findings.title: Hardcoded non-secret configuration constant
findings.message: Configuration value hardcoded instead of read from environment.

label.rationale: Hardcoded non-secret constant is a minor configurability concern, not a security risk.

findings.title: Dead assignment to unused variable
findings.message: Value assigned to a variable that is never read.

label.rationale: Dead assignment is a code-quality issue worth a low-priority cleanup ticket.

findings.title: Unreachable (dead) code after return
findings.message: Code after a return statement can never execute.

label.rationale: Dead code is low-risk maintainability debt.

findings.title: Duplicated code block
findings.message: Repeated logic that should be extracted into a shared helper.
findings.codeSnippet: # near-identical block duplicated in email() and sms()

label.rationale: Duplication is a refactoring concern; low priority.

findings.title: Unchecked return value
findings.message: Return value of a function indicating success/failure is ignored.

label.rationale: Ignoring a status return can hide failures; low-severity reliability issue.

findings.title: SQL string built from constants (no user input)
findings.message: Detected dynamic SQL construction.
findings.codeSnippet:
query = "SELECT * FROM roles WHERE name = '" + DEFAULT_ROLE + "'"
cursor.execute(query)

label.rationale: DEFAULT_ROLE is a hardcoded internal constant, not user input; no injection is possible. False positive.

findings.title: Hardcoded password in unit test fixture
findings.message: A hardcoded credential-like string was detected.

label.rationale: Dummy credential in a test fixture; not a real secret and not deployed. False positive.

findings.title: 'eval' keyword matched in non-eval context
findings.message: Detected token 'eval'.

label.rationale: 'eval' appears only inside identifier names; no eval() call exists. False positive.

findings.title: verify=False against a local mock server in tests
findings.message: requests called with verify=False.

label.rationale: verify=False targets a localhost self-signed mock in a test; not production traffic. False positive.

findings.provenance: CWE-295 (rule misfire in test against localhost)

findings.title: Placeholder API key in documentation example
findings.message: A possible API key was detected.

label.rationale: Documentation placeholder, not a real credential. False positive.

findings.title: subprocess with fixed argument list (no shell, no user input)
findings.message: Detected subprocess invocation.

label.rationale: Fixed args, shell=False, no user input; no command injection vector. False positive.

findings.provenance: CWE-78 (rule misfire on safe subprocess use)

findings.title: XSS flagged where template auto-escaping is active
findings.message: Potential XSS in template rendering.

label.rationale: Standard auto-escaping template variable with no |safe filter; output is escaped. False positive.

findings.title: MD5 used for non-security cache key
findings.message: MD5 hash usage detected.

label.rationale: MD5 used purely as a non-cryptographic cache/ETag key, not for security; acceptable use. False positive.

findings.title: Possible authorization bypass in custom middleware
findings.message: Custom authorization logic with a conditional that may permit unauthorized access in some branches.
findings.codeSnippet:
if user.is_service_account or bypass_for_path(request.path):
    return  # allow without role check

label.rationale: Whether bypass_for_path is safe depends on routing/config not visible to the scanner; needs human review before ticketing or dismissal.

findings.title: Possible secret that resolves to an environment variable
findings.message: A secret-like assignment was detected.

label.rationale: The literal default is a template placeholder, but whether it is ever interpolated to a real value at deploy time is unclear; route to human to confirm.

findings.title: Deserialization of data from an internal queue
findings.message: pickle.loads used on a message consumed from an internal queue.

label.rationale: Risk hinges on whether the queue is reachable by untrusted producers (trust boundary), which the scanner cannot determine; escalate for review.
